en.extract:
The Mongolian ground jay (Podoces hendersoni) or Henderson's ground jay is a species of bird in the family Corvidae, found in Central Asia.


Description
The bird is 28 cm (11 in) in length, with sandy-beige upperparts, off-white breast, and iridescent dark blue on the secondary feathers, and on the tips of the primary feathers. It has a long, decurved beak and a black stripe on its forehead.


Distribution
It is found in arid areas of Central Asia (Mongolia, northern China and adjacent areas of Russia and Kazakhstan). It is threatened by habitat loss and fragmentation. It tends to live on areas with gravel surfaces.


Behaviour
Eggs are typically laid and hatched in April-May. They nest in Prunus pedunculata. Females spend more time foraging and the males spend more time brooding the chicks at the start of the nesting period. 
They are most active in the morning.
Among ground jays, they have a relatively short trill call.


Diet
The main diet of the nestling Mongolian ground jay consists of common lizards, toad-headed agama, and invertebrates. It is proposed that Mongolian ground jays feed their chicks based on the availability of their food rather than in relation to the stage of the chicks development. They spend more time foraging at warmer temperatures.


References


External links

The '''Mongolian ground jay''' ('''''Podoces hendersoni''''') or '''Henderson's ground jay''' is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Corvidae]], found in Central Asia.

==Description==
The bird is {{Convert|28|cm|in|abbr=on}} in length, with sandy-beige upperparts, off-white breast, and iridescent dark blue on the secondary feathers, and on the tips of the primary feathers. It has a long, decurved beak and a black stripe on its forehead.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Madge |first=Steve |date=2020 |title=Mongolian Ground-Jay (Podoces hendersoni), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/mogjay1/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.mogjay1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

==Distribution==
It is found in arid areas of Central Asia ([[Mongolia]], northern [[China]] and adjacent areas of [[Russia]] and [[Kazakhstan]]). It is threatened by habitat loss and fragmentation.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Dashnyam |first1=Batsuuri |last2=Delgerzaya |first2=Delgerjargal |last3=Gankhuyag |first3=Purev-Ochir |last4=Amarkhuu |first4=Gungaa |date=2020 |title=Nesting Behavior of the Mongolian Ground Jay (Podoces hendersoni) in the Gobi Desert of Southern Mongolia |journal=Mongolian Journal of Biological Sciences |volume=18 |issue=1 |pages=41–46 |doi=10.22353/mjbs.2020.18.05 |issn=1684-3908|doi-access=free }}</ref> It tends to live on areas with gravel surfaces.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Londei |first=Tiziano |date=2021-04-03 |title=Birds as seed dispersers in deserts: suggestions from the ground-jays |journal=Avian Research |volume=12 |issue=1 |doi=10.1186/s40657-021-00248-7 |issn=2053-7166 |doi-access=free }}</ref>

==Behaviour==
Eggs are typically laid and hatched in April-May. They nest in ''[[Prunus pedunculata]]''. Females spend more time foraging and the males spend more time brooding the chicks at the start of the nesting period.<ref name=":0" /> 

They are most active in the morning.<ref name=":0" />

Among ground jays, they have a relatively short trill call.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Chernova |first1=O. F. |last2=Ilyashenko |first2=V. Yu. |date=August 2023 |title=The Use of the Microstructure of Covert Feathers in the Systematics of Ground Jays (Aves, Corvidae: Podoces, Eupodoces) |journal=Biology Bulletin |volume=50 |issue=4 |pages=641–655 |doi=10.1134/s1062359023601143 |bibcode=2023BioBu..50..641C |s2cid=260901099 |issn=1062-3590}}</ref>

== Diet ==
The main diet of the nestling Mongolian ground jay consists of [[Viviparous lizard|common lizards]], [[Phrynocephalus mystaceus|toad-headed agama]], and [[Invertebrate|invertebrates]]. It is proposed that Mongolian ground jays feed their chicks based on the availability of their food rather than in relation to the stage of the chicks development. They spend more time foraging at warmer temperatures.<ref name=":0" />

zh.extract:
黑尾地鸦（学名：Podoces hendersoni），是鸦科地鸦属的一种，分布于哈萨克斯坦、中国大陆和蒙古国。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
黑尾地鸦的平均体重约为120.5克。栖息地为温带荒漠，常见于稀疏的盐生灌木和半灌木内的地面。该物种的模式产地在新疆莎车。
